answer: Apply the stagger to all `review run` reviewer lens attempts after roster resolution: explicit reviewer, configured panel, and empty-panel cross-model fallback. Keep `review plan`, proposal collection commands, and the fixer out of scope.

Group across the whole review round by normalized `(engine, model, effort)` only, not by registry name. Two names with the same Claude model and effort should share one group with one lead attempt and all other attempts held.

Require byte-compatible schemas, artifact paths, attempt ID ordering, request prompt references, round summary ordering, proposal parsing, and proposal decision semantics. Allow timestamps, durations, usage values, scheduling order, and new live-output hold/release lines to differ.

Implement the stagger through a transport-agnostic first-visible-output callback. ACP should fire it on the first non-empty agent message chunk written to `stdout.log`; CLI should fire it on the first non-empty stdout or stderr write. Completion-before-output and the fixed 60-second hold timeout remain fallback releases.
Include docs updates in scope: update `docs/agents.md` to describe same-model Claude stagger behavior and update `docs/cost-budget-design.md` from planned slice to implemented behavior.
